chapter_context.summary: Laments the passing of the righteous, fiercely denounces Judah's persistent idolatrous practices, and promises healing and peace to the humble and contrite heart.

outline.text: Peaceful Passing of Righteous from Impending Evil
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Peaceful Passing of Righteous from Impending Evil (57:1-2), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Stern Exposure of Judah's Spiritual Adultery and Idolatry
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Stern Exposure of Judah's Spiritual Adultery and Idolatry (57:3-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Restoration for Contrite Heart; No Peace for Wicked
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Restoration for Contrite Heart; No Peace for Wicked (57:13-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Glory and Future Hope
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of glory and future hope, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 57, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
